reasoning: The informal claim "$f(E)$ is dense in $f(X)$" means every point of $f(X)$ lies in the closure of $f(E)$, i.e., $f(X) \subseteq \overline{f(E)}$. The Lean conclusion `f '' Set.univ ⊆ closure (f '' s)` states exactly this. The hypotheses align: `Continuous f` matches "f continuous", and `Dense s` (defined in Mathlib as `∀ x, x ∈ closure s`) matches "$E$ dense in $X$". The informal statement mentions $g$ only as part of the general setup (likely for a later part); the actual claim is purely about $f$, so omitting $g$ is appropriate. The subspace-topology reading of "dense in $f(X)$" is equivalent to $f(X) \subseteq \overline{f(E)}$ since $f(E) \subseteq f(X)$. Thus the Lean formalization is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
